LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO DEFENDANTS RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. HEARING HELD AND ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. OPPOSITION CONSIDERED. BOND REQUIREMENT OF CCP 529 WAIVED. SEE SMITH VS. ADVENTIST HEALTH SYSTEM/WEST (2010) 182 CAL.APP.4TH 729 (2010 WL 724698). ORDER GRANTING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-23-2010 Set For Order To Show Cause On Oct-18-10 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Apr-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff's Order to Show Cause to Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction. Appearances: Peter L. Ryan, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff and Moving party; Terence Hallinan, Esq. appearing for Defendant Gene Royale, and opposing party. (At the first calling of the case, only Mr. Ryan and Mr. Jay were present. Mr. Gilbert Jay, Esq. represented to the Court, that he represented Ms. Kamura, and not Mr. Hallinan. Mr. Hallinan was not present at the first calling of the matter, and came in later.) When the case was recalled, only Mr. Ryan and Mr. Hallinan were present, Mr. Jay had already departed from the courtroom.) Hearing held and argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Preliminary Injunction is Granted. Opposition considered. Bond requirement of CCP 529 waived. See Smith vs. Adventist Health System/West (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 729 (2010 WL 724698). Order granting preliminary injunction signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Carlito Mandia, CSR # 9689
